# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Project Overview
Beudox HR is a comprehensive Human Resources Management System built as a React TypeScript single-page application. It provides organizations with tools to manage employees, projects, evaluations, payroll, and HR policies through a modern web interface.

## Architecture & Technology Stack

### Frontend Framework
- **React 18** with functional components and hooks
- **TypeScript** for type safety
- **Vite** as build tool and development server
- **React Router DOM** for client-side routing

### UI & Styling
- **Shadcn/ui** component library built on Radix UI primitives
- **Tailwind CSS** for utility-first styling
- **Lucide React** for icons
- **Responsive design** with mobile-first approach

### State Management & Data Fetching
- **TanStack Query (React Query)** for server state management
- **Supabase client** for database operations and real-time subscriptions
- **React Hook Form** with Zod validation for forms

### Rich Text & Advanced Features
- **Tiptap** for rich text editing in HR policies
- **Recharts** for data visualization
- **React Image Crop** for profile picture management
- **Date-fns** for date manipulation

## Application Structure

### Routing & Pages
The application uses a protected route system with role-based access control:

- **Public Routes**: `/login`, `/forgot-password`
- **Protected Routes**: All other routes require authentication
- **Role-based Access**: Different user roles (ceo, hr_manager, team_lead, employee) have different permissions

Key page routes:
- `/dashboard` - Main dashboard
- `/employees` - Employee management
- `/projects` - Project management
- `/evaluations` - Performance evaluations
- `/payroll` - Payroll processing
- `/hr-policies` - HR policy documents
- `/settings` - System configuration

### Component Architecture

#### Layout Components
- **AppLayout**: Main application layout with sidebar navigation
- **AppSidebar**: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- **TopBar**: Top navigation bar with user menu and notifications

#### Feature Components
- **EvaluationTimeline**: Displays historical evaluation data
- **SearchableEmployeeSelect**: Employee selection with search functionality
- **RichTextEditor**: WYSIWYG editor for policy documents
- **Settings Tabs**: Modular settings management (Company, Departments, Roles, etc.)

#### UI Components
Extensive use of shadcn/ui components:
- Form controls (Input, Select, Button, etc.)
- Layout components (Card, Tabs, Dialog, etc.)
- Data display (Table, Badge, Avatar, etc.)
- Feedback (Toast, Alert, Skeleton, etc.)

## Data Model & Business Logic

### Core Entities
- **Employees**: User profiles with roles, departments, contact info
- **Companies**: Multi-tenant organization data
- **Projects**: Project management with team assignments
- **Clients**: Client relationship management
- **Evaluations**: Performance reviews (quarterly and daily)
- **Payroll**: Salary processing and payslip generation
- **Invoices**: Client billing and payment tracking
- **HR Policies**: Document management with rich text content

### Authentication & Authorization
- **Supabase Auth** for user authentication
- **Role-based permissions** implemented in `src/lib/role-access.ts`
- **Route protection** with automatic redirects based on user roles
- **Session management** with loading states and error handling

### Data Flow Patterns
- **Server State**: Managed by TanStack Query with caching and background updates
- **Real-time Updates**: Supabase subscriptions for live data synchronization
- **Optimistic Updates**: Immediate UI feedback for better user experience
- **Error Handling**: Comprehensive error boundaries and user feedback

## Key Files & Directories

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing

### Core Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking logic
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/integrations/supabase/`: Database client and type definitions

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Styling configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

### Testing
- `src/test/`: Unit tests with Vitest
- `playwright.config.ts`: E2E testing configuration

## Development Patterns

### Component Patterns
- **Functional components** with TypeScript interfaces
- **Custom hooks** for reusable logic
- **Compound components** for complex UI elements
- **Forward refs** for imperative APIs

### Data Fetching
- **Query keys** following TanStack Query conventions
- **Enabled conditions** to prevent unnecessary requests
- **Error handling** with user-friendly messages
- **Loading states** with skeleton components

### Form Handling
- **React Hook Form** for form state management
- **Zod schemas** for validation
- **Controlled components** with proper TypeScript types
- **Form submission** with loading states and error handling

## Database Schema

The application uses Supabase (PostgreSQL) with the following key tables:
- `employees` - User profiles and organizational data
- `companies` - Multi-tenant organization information
- `evaluations` - Quarterly performance reviews
- `daily_evaluations` - Daily feedback and ratings
- `projects` - Project management data
- `clients` - Client relationship data
- `invoices` - Billing and payment records
- `payroll` - Salary processing data
- `hr_policies` - Policy document storage

## Performance Considerations

- **Code splitting** with Vite's dynamic imports
- **Lazy loading** for route components
- **Query caching** with TanStack Query
- **Image optimization** with proper sizing and formats
- **Bundle analysis** for optimizing build size

## Security

- **Row Level Security (RLS)** in Supabase database
- **Authentication required** for all protected routes
- **Role-based access control** at component and route levels
- **Input validation** with Zod schemas
- **Secure environment variables** for API keys

## Deployment

- **Static site generation** with Vite build
- **CDN deployment** (Vercel, Netlify, etc.)
- **Environment-specific builds** with Vite modes
- **Database migrations** managed through Supabase

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the codebase structure, patterns, and implementation details of the Beudox HR application.
